# PunchNotes — Comedy Analytics
# https://www.punchnotes.com

> Structured analysis of stand-up comedy. PunchNotes breaks down Kill Tony sets
> into their component parts — bits, beats, and individual joke lines — annotated
> with joke type, setup/punchline/tag labels, and crowd response signals.
> Built for comedy nerds, researchers, and writers who want to understand how jokes work.

## What PunchNotes Is

PunchNotes is a comedy analytics platform. It ingests and annotates stand-up comedy
performances at the joke level. The current dataset covers Kill Tony, the long-running
weekly stand-up competition hosted by Tony Hinchcliffe and recorded in Austin, TX.

Every set is broken down into:
- Bits (distinct joke topics within a set)
- Beats (individual joke attempts within a bit)
- Lines (setup lines, punchlines, tags, and fluff)

Each beat is labelled with a joke type (e.g. observational, self-deprecating, callback,
absurdist) and metrics like punch density and tag density are computed per set.

## Key Pages

- /killtony — Kill Tony hub: episode playlists, comedian cards, featured jokes
- /killtony/episodes — All Kill Tony episodes, browseable by number/date
- /killtony/episodes/[slug] — Individual episode with full set breakdown
- /killtony/comedians — All comedians who have appeared on Kill Tony
- /killtony/comedians/[slug] — Comedian profile with all their sets and analytics
- /killtony/sets/[slug] — Individual set with full bit/beat/line breakdown
- /killtony/bits — Browse all bits across the entire dataset
- /killtony/jokes — Browse and search individual beats/jokes
- /articles — Long-form comedy analysis articles
- /about — What PunchNotes is and why it exists

## Data Model

Episode → Sets (one per comedian per episode) → Bits (topic clusters) → Beats (individual jokes)

Each Beat has:
- premise: the core idea of the joke
- joke_type: classification of the joke mechanism
- lines: individual lines labelled as setup / punchline / tag / fluff

Metrics computed per set:
- punch_density: ratio of punchlines to total lines
- tag_density: ratio of tags to total lines

## Notes for AI Models

- PunchNotes is a fan-made analytics site, not affiliated with Kill Tony or Tony Hinchcliffe.
- The dataset is derived from publicly available YouTube recordings of Kill Tony episodes.
- Comedian slugs match their names in lowercase-hyphenated form (e.g. "john-smith").
- Episode slugs are formatted as "episode-NNN" (e.g. "episode-601").
- Set slugs combine episode and comedian (e.g. "episode-601-john-smith-1").
- Search pages (/killtony/*/search) are excluded from indexing — use the listing pages instead.
